reviews.review_description: Best game of the three Easily definitely worth the $10. The rancor is a cool ad and the part where you have to climb is a cool ad. I also like becoming Darth Vader sidekick for a short time. The only thing I would add is if you could go back in time at the end, and stopped at Darth Vader ￼ for getting the power star.￼ Or whatever it’s called and it would be a cool add-on but besides that it’s a good game ￼

reviews.review_title: A Short but Brilliant VR Star Wars Exper
reviews.review_description:
I played through all three episodes of the Vader Immortal trilogy, and honestly, I really enjoyed it. Yes, each part is pretty short on its own, but experiencing them together as one complete story feels very satisfying. I took my time exploring the environments and ended up spending about two hours across all three episodes. The locations are gorgeous, and the atmosphere pulls you right in.
Just a heads-up: this isn’t an action-heavy game. It’s more of an interactive cinematic experience, but it’s executed really well, with some genuinely epic moments. The lightsaber mechanics are a real highlight - training, swinging the blade, and deflecting blaster bolts feel incredibly satisfying and immersive in VR, even though the combat stays simple and accessible.
I’m not a hardcore Star Wars fan these days, but I grew up watching the films and loved them. Getting to step back into that universe in VR after all these years was a real treat. Standing face-to-face with Vader actually gave me chills.
At full price, the length might feel a bit brief for some, but on sale, the complete trilogy is an absolute steal. If you’re looking for a polished, atmospheric VR adventure that lets you live out a few iconic Star Wars moments, this is absolutely worth picking up.

Motion sickness: No problems here.  Teleport controls and no forced movement.  Snap turning and no vertical movement aside from climbing manually. 

The game itself is so dark I couldn't make out was happening half the time.  It would have been great otherwise.  

I tried adjusting settings and used Google and AI but could not find a solution.

** MY OPINION (complete trilogy): A HUGE FAN FAVORITE! ** EPIC, GRANDIOSE, MASTERFUL… AND ABOVE ALL, INCREDIBLY IMMERSIVE! Visually stunning, Vader Immortal impresses with its lighting effects, iconic environments, legendary sound effects (the lightsaber), and a soundtrack worthy of the films. It's incredibly easy to pick up and play, fully customizable, and motion sickness-free: a perfect entry point into VR, even for beginners. 🔴 Episode I: brilliant setup, the first encounter with Darth Vader is impressive! 🔴 Episode II: shorter but packed with revelations and iconic scenes! 🔴 Episode III: intense and memorable finale, a truly apotheosis! SHORT BUT INTENSE Each episode is relatively short (45–60 minutes if you take your time), but the DOJOS change everything. They offer tremendous replayability! Up to 3 hours per dojo, with progressively increasing difficulty, and combined use of the lightsaber AND the Force in the final episode. 😈 MEET DARTH VADER IN VR! Seeing him, fighting alongside him, then facing him… it's an emotional rollercoaster and an experience every Star Wars fan simply must have. This trilogy alone justifies buying a Quest for any fan of the saga. A childhood dream come true. THE PROS - THE CONS Total Star Wars immersion. Excellent VR comfort. Accessible to beginners. Ultra-fun and long-lasting dojos. Darth Vader… simply amazing! But the main adventure is rather short. We always want more 😉 🎯 CONCLUSION: AN ABSOLUTE HIT! Vader Immortal – The Trilogy is an unmissable VR experience, to be played in one sitting. A narrative gem, spectacular and emotional, designed for both fans and newcomers to VR.

as long as you know youre getting more of an experience than a full game, granted the jedi dojo at the end that you unlock is fun and the most game like thing about this, then you wont be disappointed...

My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.

With the version 83 OS release Meta has fixed whatever they broke in their previous OS  release and Vader Immortal is working on Quest 3 now.

While I'm glad I can play this VR classic again, it's disconcerting that Meta and / or game developers can take away games you've purchased at any time with buggy software updates.

Note that the developer responses to bug reports are worthless. If you send an email to the address given you get a reply back saying that they don't support the Quest version and to contact Meta.

So no one maintains Vader Immortal's code.

It's abandonware at the mercy of Meta's ever changing buggy OS.

Wow what a VR experience!

reviews.review_title: Enhances the experience with the Force.
reviews.review_description:
Even though this is the very first series of Star Wars VR games, the game was professionally developed as it harbours great quality in many aspects. The overall quality is very high, and it never disappoints Star Wars fans.

The graphics are highly detailed. The sound and music are epic, as all Star Wars media should be. The atmosphere, the motion, the special effects, the environment, the scene transitions, the feeling—everything looks great. They truly draw out all the essences of Star Wars and put them together in this VR experience. This is what a Star Wars VR game is meant to be.

Although the story length in each episode is quite short, it is very enjoyable. In this Episode 2, the story continues as the protagonist starts learning to wield the Force, and uses his newly learned special powers to survive in worsening situations.

The Lightsabre Dojo mode in this Episode 2 begins to incorporate Force powers into the action. The playstyle becomes different, as the player cannot just rely on his lightsabre expertise intensively honed from the previous episode, but needs to maximise the benefit of the available Force powers and the surrounding environment to pass through the trials.

Nothing works

reviews.review_description: the game is great but sadly the loading is to long so i cant play

It seems like a good game at first, but then again whenever I turn (and sometimes when I don't) the game just goes crazy and I'm just turning without even touching the controllers constantly. Also, when I was following Vader right at the start, the game somehow made me teleport inside his cape and after I got out this black outline of a cage or something was just hovering next to Vader until he went away and then when I saw the droid and the alien again they were both just black outlines apart from the alien's gun and the droids crystal trinket thing. Then whenever I activated my lightsaber a white image of the droid was just on the end for some reason. Please fix these glitches. And I got to the bit where the droid is being almost eaten by and in the hand of the giant monster, and I couldn't figure out what to do so the game "helpfully" showed some blue arrows pointing at a solid wall and said grab. I tried. It did nothing. Please do fix the glitches.

So this was an immediate purchase after completing the first episode without hesitation. It continues teaching you new skills however lacks a little engagement compared to the first.  Yet again impressive but short still worth the cost.

reviews.review_title: Update has broken the game on Quest 2
reviews.review_description: Sadly, some update, either the Meta OS, or the app itself, has made the game lock up for several seconds at a time, every few seconds, rendering it unusable.  It has been this way for nearly 6 months now and there is no way to report the issue that I can find.  This is devastating for me because it was my #1 workout app and now I can no longer play it on my Quest 2. This applies to all 3 episodes, and with no way of reporting this to the dev team, I can no longer recommend this app.
If you HATE the teleport locomotion then do NOT get this game, because the walking locomotion is BROKEN, it just won't save

This episode focuses more on the force power than on the lightsaber which is cool but once again, it is roughly 30'. You are still stationary while playing and the same mechanics: walking to a specific position, cutscene where you cannot move and you just wait. The story gets way better, the graphics as well and it reminded me of Behemoth with the monster but it is still a fraction of a game.

I am hoping the third episode matches or exceeds the first one.

reviews.review_title: Please please don't waste your money
reviews.review_description: One of the worst VR games I've ever played the other 2 episodes being the others, not a game just a walk through and a bad story that lasts about 25 mins, you will be devastated you wasted your time and money on this truly abysmal VR experience posing as a game honestly I wouldn't pay more than 0.99 for it knowing what I know now. Please just don't do it.

Really? 10 minutes of playing for 10
 dollars? Even hookers are cheaper. Disney murdered the entire world of star wars.

- Each episode is less than 30min.
- The story is boring and the characters annoying.
- Yes, it's got light saber. But parry left, parry right, parry above, stab, parry left, parry right, parry above, stab. So combat is boring as hell. In episode 2 there's force powers as well, but the combat here is either the same as before, some blocking of bolts and some smashing of enemies. They swarm you, always go to the same positions. Movement is practically nonexistent.
- The only positive thing to say is, that you can return the whole thing, as the play time ia short enough.
Even tho the first 1 is better this one focuses more on the use of the force , it has its scarry moments , epic fights and a good storyline

Pro's
The graphics are great story on 1 and part 2 are great.
Con's
Way too short both of them were not worth 10 bucks each. Probably wont replay it so won't get the 3rd one because it's probably just as short as the first 2 and if i hadn't bought them at the same time i probably wouldn't have gotten the 2nd one even though i did enjoy it until it ended and maybe 30 minutes. You should have put them all together at least or made them free to play then when we were done we could just delete them.

This simply is a terrible game; including 2 and 3. You spend almost the entire 1st game walking around listening to exposition and you will get some training with a lightsaber, to leave you wanting more for 2 and 3. In 2 you get to use the force and you will use it surprisingly easy for someone that just learned that they can even use it. You will predominantly use the force and can even not use the lightsaber for the rest of the game and 3. Finally in 3, you may believe it has been set up to battle 100s of stormtroopers and have a final showdown with Darth Vader, and you do but on a mini scale. As the battle is taking place you can force throw the small amount of stormtroopers trying to engage you. And at the end have a 1 minute fight with Vader, which was incredibly lame. The story overall is absolutely terrible, and whould have liked to see me growing as Vaders padawan, instead him showing me how to use the force then go on a 2 episode side quest, just to have a lame 1 minute battle against him at the end. The game is too easy, and I can't see how anyone can care about the Mustafarians. This is grade A Disney s***, that ruined the sequel trilogy, and has now had a horrific start to VR. DONT BUY IT. Not even on discount. Or buy it and see what I'm saying. Also it's maybe an hour long.
This was like 10 mins of gameplay...
